query: What is the current level of crude oil in the US Strategic Petroleum Reserve as of June 2026, per EIA weekly data (WCSSTUS1)? What has been the weekly trajectory over the past 3-6 months (fills or releases)?
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's a summary of the key findings on the US Strategic Petroleum Reserve:

**Current Level:** As of the most recent EIA weekly data (week ending **July 3, 2026**), the SPR held **319.489 million barrels** — the lowest level since ~1983, down ~20.7% year-over-year.

**Trajectory (past 3–6 months):** The pattern shows a sharp regime change:
- **Jan–mid-March 2026:** Stable/plateaued at ~415.4 million barrels
- **Late March 2026 onward:** A steep, sustained drawdown began, running ~5.5–9.9 million barrels/week (~0.8–1.4 million bbl/day at peak), for 14+ consecutive weeks. Total drawn: ~96 million barrels from peak.

**Driver:** This is a deliberate **release**, not a passive drop. On March 11, 2026, the US committed to release **172 million barrels** as part of a coordinated IEA release of 400 million barrels to counter Iran-war supply disruptions. DOE issued an RFP for the first tranche (up to 86 million barrels) on March 13, 2026.

June 2026 weekly readings specifically: June 5 (349.2M), June 12 (340.3M), June 19 (331.2M), June 26 (325.7M).

Full data points, sources, and the structured payload have been submitted.

key_facts: US SPR held 319.489 million barrels as of the week ending July 3, 2026 (EIA series WCSSTUS1) — the lowest since ~1983.
SPR is down ~20.7% year-over-year (from ~402.76M barrels one year prior) and down ~96M barrels since the late-March 2026 peak of ~415.4M.
The reserve was essentially flat/plateaued at ~415.4M barrels from mid-February through mid-March 2026, continuing a prior refill posture.
A sharp, sustained drawdown began the week of March 27, 2026 and continued for 14+ consecutive weeks through early July.
June 2026 weekly levels: June 5: 349.2M; June 12: 340.3M; June 19: 331.2M; June 26: 325.7M barrels.
Peak weekly draws ran ~5.5–9.9 million barrels per week (~0.8–1.4 million bbl/day) during May–June 2026.
The drawdown is driven by a US pledge to release 172 million barrels as part of a coordinated IEA release of 400 million barrels to counter Iran-war supply disruptions (announced March 11, 2026).
DOE issued an RFP March 13, 2026 for the first tranche of up to 86 million barrels via emergency exchange.
Mid-June 2026 media (CNBC, Reuters, Bloomberg) reported the SPR at its lowest level in more than 40 years.

data_points.metric: US SPR release commitment (IEA coordinated program)
First tranche SPR emergency exchange RFP
